name: Baked Crab, Pepper and Spinach Frittata
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Fresh Spinach (1 Lg.Bunch), wash, stems removed
2/3 cup Asiago Or Parmesan Cheese, coarsely grated
8 oz Dungeness Crab Meat, well picked over
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a pan over moderate heat. Add onion and garlic; sauté until soft but not brown.
2. Blanch spinach in lightly salted boiling water for a few seconds until wilted. Drain and immediately plunge into ice water to stop cooking and set color. Drain again and gently squeeze out moisture with a clean tea towel. Coarsely chop spinach and combine with onion mixture. Season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.
3. Char peppers over an open flame or under a broiler until all sides are charred. Remove and cover loosely with plastic for a few minutes to sweat. Then scrape off the charred skin with the point of a knife. Remove seeds and stems; cut into long, thick slices.
4. Butter or oil a small terrine or loaf pan (8x4x2 1/2 inches) and line the bottom with buttered waxed paper or parchment.
5. Beat eggs briefly with herbs; season with salt and pepper. Layer one third of spinach mixture on the bottom of the pan, followed by one third of roasted red peppers, one third of cheese, and one third of crab. Pour about one third of egg mixture over the layers and gently tap the pan to evenly distribute.
6. Continue layering in this order: spinach mixture, roasted red peppers, cheese, crab, egg mixture. Cover with parchment or foil and bake at 325°F until eggs are just set. Remove from water bath and let rest for 15-20 minutes before unmolding if serving warm.
Yield: 6 servings
